area: Runner sync / steward lifecycle / closeout UX
finding: After a runner-sync cache-hit build published a receipt for the previous sealed source, the steward queue kept that completed source at queue head. Enqueuing the next sealed source for the same task then landed at position 2 and required the operator to manually compute the receipt digest and release the old steward work before the new build could proceed.
expectedBehavior: Runner-sync should expose a first-layer, copyable release-or-advance command after build publication, and should consider an automatic same-task advancement path when the current queue-head receipt is already present and digest-verifiable. The lifecycle should be generic across tasks and must not depend on manual SHA/digest spelunking.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0196 closeout on 2026-07-20, `runner-sync-216fd2a4` completed for sealed source `f0625e71379741f7d27e64c85aa62784837e7c5a`. After the delivery commit `302d8b806c42b64a4f9e266905e3b159a1c8d7a0`, `broker runner-sync enqueue` returned `waiting-different-source` at position 2 for `runner-sync-6dcbbea1` until the operator ran `Get-FileHash` and `broker runner-sync release --steward-work-id runner-sync-216fd2a4 ...` manually.
followUp: Add runner-sync lifecycle tests for same-task sequential sealed sources, emit digest-ready release commands from build/close preflight output, and investigate safe auto-release when the recorded receipt matches the queue-head steward work.
